title: College of Charleston Facilities Tour
body: I applied back in December for Fall semester of 2016. It is my number one choice! I love the campus and the opportunities to achieve academically. I look forward to becoming a more independent individual and learn from people and their ideas so I can become a better person and leader. Thank you for reviewing my application!
author: @markdonohue1100

title: College of Charleston Facilities Tour
body: I'm only a junior is HS now, but this school is definitely in my top three! I can't wait to visit this fall!

body: Seems like in 2024 I would be a transfer student at COC so I’m looking forward to possibly transferred there but I’m trying to get more information about the college. Also just wanted to let you know that you’re very pretty so can’t believe you’re single thanks for this video.

title: things you NEED to know about the College Of Charleston..
body: Just spent 2 years at a college in LA and I'm so sick of it so I applied to transfer to Charleston in the fall lol! Very helpful video 🤍

title: things you NEED to know about the College Of Charleston..
body: Hi Teagan! I just commented on one of your other vids about how I might be going to cofc as a communications major too next year!!!
author: @samantharittenberg9652

title: things you NEED to know about the College Of Charleston..
body: I would have to very strongly disagree with the fact that “majority of the men you’d see on the street are gay or bi.” I am a gay man and the community is incredibly small and isolating here, I’d say 10-15% of the men here are gay but even that is really pushing it. And then putting it into perspective, only 33% of the school is males so that makes the population so much smaller then it already is. I may be biased because I’m part of the community but even then there’s a lot more gay and bi women here than men.
author: @broadwaylover3105

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: 4:39. As someone who has ADHD, the most relatable moment in this video is this. Laying in bed with nothing, but your thoughts replaying over and over
author: @shabubbalicious

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: “It’s not like I’m doing it on purpose” That hit me hard. I can’t tell you how many times I have said that in my life.
author: @hayleyypricee

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: The brain being active and fully concentrating during basketball game(source of dopamine) and rethinking about things heard throughout the day before sleep is so real
author: @dnki6767

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: Teacher gets mad at kid not focusing when there is a girl sitting 5 feet away talking on her phone
author: @sillythygoose

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: Ok but why ain't homegirl at 1:56 getting in trouble for having a full blown phone call in the middle of class. I'd be staring at her too.
author: @rinyaskyline

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: my dad has ADHD, and probably i have too, bc i really identified with that part 3:52 , it happens with me, and they say: "focus on what I'm saying" and i really try..
author: @saphir4x

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body:
Me trying to focus in classes*
Also me: Fidgets with a pen while staring out the window while thinking about something that happened years ago
author: @naydsoe27

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: god the part where he gets frustrated after his mom says hes not trying....... kinda felt that a lil too much........ ouch :(
author: @MiKUiSSTiLLALiVE

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: This video helped me a lot to understand how people with ADHD feel. My father has ADHD, every time I talk to him about my interests, how I did in school, etc., I feel like he doesn't listen to me and I get upset at him, but now that I'm 13 I'm mature enough to try to understand him. I am trying my best to research ADHD to help him and know what to do in those situations without getting angry. He truly is a great dad and deserves to be understood like every other person  with ADHD :) ❤
author: @Meowcidal

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: this is so accurate. especially the way having like 5 thoughts at once is portrayed with squares on the screen. everything runs through your head so fast that it all feels muted and foggy
author: @isamccain

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: What many neurotypical people also don't understand is that the fidgeting actually helps us focus. So if they tell you to "just sit still" they're actually making it more difficult to concentrate
author: @jado627

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: 5:13 I hate that ring sm like it doesn’t help that I alr have bad nighttime anxiety but that ring makes it like 10x worse its scary to think it’s not just me who goes through this
author: @JakeFromStateFarm-jh8oi

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body:
she: "just concentrate on what I'm saying, can you do that?"
My brain:
can you do that can you do that can you do that can you do that can you do that
author: @leticiabosco9175

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: The scary part is the cuts in time. My life has flown by and continues to fly by. Days feel like minutes unless im bored or off my meds.
author: @johnwilliams919

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: 2:02 Happens to me every time someone's calling me out, my mind literally puts the first sentence on a loop out of a bizarre mix of Astonishment and frustration
author: @alessandrovisco1489

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: The repeating part and random memories popping up is very real, it's like your memory was just shuffled like a deck of cards and then you looked at all of them. Nothings in order. And you keep getting lost
author: @kidredglow2060

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body:
"Just try"
"I am"
"No you're not" <---- I HATE this response so much. For real do you thing that we do this on purpose?? It's so stressful when we don't meet the expectation the people want us to be at. It's like, they can do it so why can't I?? We know what we are supposed to do it's just difficult for us to fulfill what is expected of us. We aren't stupid.
author: @lightofhaley03

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: This is actually one of the closest representations of what it's like to have ADHD done through editing, I hope people can start to be more understanding with it
author: @yagomateo2815

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body:
The “you’re not trying” cuts like a knife other favorites include
“you’re just making excuses”
and “if you actually cared it wouldn’t be so hard”
author: @ZeppelinPietrPryor

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: 5:02 Very true! Instead of one voice, I have a whole bunch of thoughts playing in my head. It's like I have layers of thoughts in my head. I have two Michael Jackson songs playing at the same time, some random lyrics and about 3-4 events that happened that day (not necessarily that one, but most often like this) The only thing that SOMETIMES helps me concentrate for a few minutes is lofi girl music
author: @Green-px5mv

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body: i felt it when his mom said “all i asked was to get milk” i have a bad habit of remembering certain things, & remembering right after .. it’s bad 😭
author: @ladilopez7010

title: A Day in the Life with ADHD
body:
"I'm trying !"
"No, no you're not."

That one hit hard. It's so hard for me to remember things and register when someone is talking to me, and I really do try.

title: DAY IN THE LIFE W ELI ELLIS AT COLLEGE OF CHARLESTON‼️‼️🔥🔥
body: I don't mind NIL but i really have to get used to guys being so d*** worried about where or whats next. I feel these kids have such a hard time being involved in the moment. before they know it, more than half these kids will be wondering where time went, and may end up asking themselves why in the world did I invest so much time in something that didn't really change me for the better. Basketball only lasts so long
author: @Boreyheng223
